project.name: hr-interview
project.description: AI-powered HR Interview system built with Next.js, React, Prisma, Google Gemini AI. Handles job descriptions, candidate management, CV parsing, matching, and AI-driven interviews.

nodes.name: eslint.config.mjs
nodes.summary: ESLint configuration combining Next.js core-web-vitals and TypeScript presets with custom global ignores.

nodes.name: next.config.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal Next.js configuration file with empty config options placeholder.

nodes.name: scrape-nova-colors.mjs
nodes.summary: Puppeteer script that scrapes novagroup.vn to extract color palette data (backgrounds, text colors, button styles) for design reference.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Launches headless browser, navigates to novagroup.vn, extracts computed color styles from all elements and button-like elements.

nodes.name: test-tts.mjs
nodes.summary: Puppeteer UI test verifying TTS (text-to-speech) and mouth animation behavior during AI interview, checking that avatar animation stops when speech ends.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Tests TTS playback and avatar mouth animation sync on the interview page, polling speechSynthesis state and avatar border classes.

nodes.name: test-debug.mjs
nodes.summary: Debug Puppeteer script for TTS-TICK logging, monitoring text streaming cursor and speech synthesis state on the interview page.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Captures TTS-TICK console logs and polls speech/cursor state to debug text streaming timing.

nodes.name: test-final.mjs
nodes.summary: Final Puppeteer UI test measuring text-speech synchronization smoothness by tracking word-by-word text growth steps during TTS playback.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Tracks blinking cursor and text growth at 300ms intervals, reports total growth steps and whether text-speech sync is smooth.

nodes.name: test-sourcing.mjs
nodes.summary: Puppeteer test for the candidate sourcing flow: creates a new JD, submits it, and waits for sourcing progress to complete with generated candidates.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Automates JD creation form, monitors sourcing progress bar, and verifies candidate table is populated.

nodes.name: test-stream-sync.mjs
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Puppeteer test for text streaming and TTS synchronization, capturing console logs, tracking avatar state, and verifying mouth animation, cursor, and message rendering.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Full integration test checking TTS logs, text streaming progression screenshots, and final state verification of mouth animation, cursor, and message count.

nodes.name: test-sync-detail.mjs
nodes.summary: Detailed text-speech sync analysis script sampling at 500ms intervals, tracking text growth during speech and computing sync quality metrics.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Collects time-series samples of text length vs speech state, analyzes growth steps and text growth during speech to determine sync quality.

nodes.name: test-sync-v3.mjs
nodes.summary: V3 sync test injecting a probe via evaluateOnNewDocument to intercept 80ms TTS tick interval, producing raw stream logs for fine-grained text growth analysis.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Patches window.setInterval to wrap 80ms TTS tick, collects __streamLog entries, and analyzes raw growth steps for streaming smoothness.

nodes.name: postcss.config.mjs
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration enabling the @tailwindcss/postcss plugin for TailwindCSS processing.

nodes.name: prisma.config.ts
nodes.summary: Prisma configuration file that defines schema path and migrations directory using dotenv for environment loading.

nodes.name: seed.ts
nodes.summary: Database seed script that creates default admin, recruiter, and candidate users with bcrypt-hashed passwords via Prisma upsert.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Async seed function that iterates over predefined users, hashes passwords, and upserts them into the database.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side candidate detail page showing score breakdown, matched/missing skills, CV summary, and an AI interview activation button with loading overlay.

nodes.name: ScoreBar
nodes.summary: Reusable progress bar component rendering a labeled score indicator with configurable color.

nodes.name: ScoreBadge
nodes.summary: Circular badge component that displays a numeric score with color-coded background based on thresholds.

nodes.name: CandidateDetailPage
nodes.summary: Main page component that fetches candidate from static or dynamic sourcing store, displays score breakdown, skills, CV summary, and triggers AI interview.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side form page for creating a new candidate with Zod validation and react-hook-form, posting to /api/candidates.

nodes.name: NewCandidatePage
nodes.summary: Form page component using react-hook-form with Zod schema to collect candidate info (name, email, phone, source, notes) and submit via POST /api/candidates.

nodes.name: onSubmit
nodes.summary: Form submission handler that POSTs candidate data to API and redirects on success.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side candidates sourcing page with tabbed JD view, filtering/sorting/search, score badges, and AI interview launch capability for both static and dynamic candidates.

nodes.name: ScoreBadge
nodes.summary: Score badge component with color-coded thresholds for the candidates table.

nodes.name: CandidatesSourcingPage
nodes.summary: Main candidates list page with JD tabs (static + dynamic from sourcing store), search/filter/sort controls, and a table with interview launch actions.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Server-rendered dashboard overview page displaying recruitment stats cards and a recent interviews table with mock data.

nodes.name: DashboardPage
nodes.summary: Dashboard page rendering 4 stat cards (jobs, candidates, interviews, avg score) and a recent interviews table.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side AI interview session page with video avatar panel, chat interface, TTS text-stream sync, and interview lifecycle management via Zustand store.

nodes.name: InterviewSessionPage
nodes.summary: Interview session page managing interview lifecycle (start, question, answer, close), TTS sync with text streaming, video avatar panel, and chat interface.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side interview report page displaying the AI-generated report card and full transcript with print support.

nodes.name: InterviewReportPage
nodes.summary: Report page that renders interview report card and transcript from Zustand store, redirecting if no report data exists.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Server-rendered interviews list page showing a searchable table of mock interviews with status badges and score display.

nodes.name: InterviewsPage
nodes.summary: Interviews list page rendering a table of interviews with search, filter controls, and status/score columns using mock data.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side job description edit form with Zod validation, react-hook-form, employment type select, and interview config (duration, question count), submitting via PUT /api/jds/[id].

nodes.name: EditJobPage
nodes.summary: Edit JD form page that loads mock data, populates react-hook-form fields, and submits updates via PUT /api/jds/[id].

nodes.name: onSubmit
nodes.summary: Form submission handler that PUTs updated JD data to API and redirects to job detail page.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side job detail page showing JD info, requirements, and interview settings in tabbed layout. Uses mock data with TODO markers for real API integration.

nodes.name: JobDetailPage
nodes.summary: Default export React component rendering job detail view with parse, edit, delete actions and tabbed content (info, requirements, settings).

nodes.name: handleParse
nodes.summary: Async handler to trigger AI parsing of the JD (currently mock).

nodes.name: handleDelete
nodes.summary: Async handler to delete a job position with confirmation (currently mock).

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side form page for creating a new job description with Zod validation and react-hook-form. Triggers AI candidate sourcing via sourcing store on submit.

nodes.name: NewJobPage
nodes.summary: Default export React component rendering new JD creation form with title, department, location, employment type, description, and raw JD text fields.

nodes.name: onSubmit
nodes.summary: Form submit handler that triggers AI candidate sourcing via sourcing store and redirects to candidates page.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side jobs listing page displaying job positions in a searchable table with status badges and action dropdown menus. Uses mock data.

nodes.name: JobsPage
nodes.summary: Default export React component rendering searchable table of job positions with view, edit, parse, and archive actions.

nodes.name: formatDate
nodes.summary: Formats ISO date string to Vietnamese locale date.

nodes.name: handleArchive
nodes.summary: Async handler to archive a job position (currently mock).

nodes.name: handleParse
nodes.summary: Async handler to trigger AI parsing of a JD (currently mock).

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard route group layout composing sidebar, header, and main content area using SidebarProvider pattern.

nodes.name: DashboardLayout
nodes.summary: Default export server component layout wrapping children with AppSidebar, AppHeader, and SidebarProvider.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Reports dashboard page showing summary stats cards and tabbed chart placeholders for interview analytics. Currently uses static mock data.

nodes.name: ReportsPage
nodes.summary: Default export component rendering reports page with summary stat cards and tabbed sections for overview, by-position, and trend analytics.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: NextAuth catch-all API route re-exporting GET and POST handlers from the centralized auth config.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: User registration API route with Zod validation, bcrypt password hashing, duplicate email check, and Prisma user creation.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: POST handler for user registration: validates input with Zod, checks email uniqueness, hashes password with bcrypt, creates user via Prisma.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route to re-parse an existing CV with auth/RBAC checks, delegating to cv-service parseExistingCv.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: POST handler to re-parse existing CV: checks auth/permissions, delegates to parseExistingCv service.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route for listing candidate CVs (GET) and uploading new CV files with size/type validation (POST). Uses auth, RBAC, and cv-service.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: GET handler listing CVs for a candidate with auth/RBAC, querying Prisma with skills included.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: POST handler for CV file upload with auth, RBAC, file size (10MB) and type (PDF/TXT) validation, delegating to uploadAndParseCv service.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: API route to calculate match score between a candidate's parsed CV and a job's parsed JD using the matching engine service.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: GET handler calculating match score: validates auth/RBAC, fetches latest parsed CV and job parsed data, calls calculateMatch service.
REST API route for single candidate operations: GET (view), PUT (update with Zod validation), DELETE (soft delete/withdraw). Auth + RBAC enforced via requirePermission.
nodes.name: route.ts
GET
nodes.summary: Fetch single candidate by ID with view:candidates permission check.

nodes.name: PUT
nodes.summary: Update candidate fields with Zod validation and manage:candidates permission.

nodes.name: DELETE
nodes.summary: Soft-delete candidate (set WITHDRAWN status) with manage:candidates permission.
REST API route for candidate collection: GET (paginated list with filters) and POST (create new candidate). Auth + RBAC enforced. Handles unique email conflict (409).
nodes.name: route.ts
GET
nodes.summary: Paginated candidate list with page/limit/status/search/jdId filters.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Create new candidate with Zod validation, returns 409 on duplicate email.
Health check endpoint: checks API, database (Prisma raw query), and Redis (ping). Returns 200 ok or 503 degraded.
nodes.name: route.ts
GET
nodes.summary: Health check: tests database and Redis connectivity, returns aggregated status.
Interview chat endpoint: receives candidate answer, evaluates it via AI, generates AI spoken response with feedback and transition to next question.
nodes.name: route.ts
POST
nodes.summary: Evaluate candidate answer and generate AI response using interview-ai-service.
Interview report generation endpoint: takes Q&A with scores, generates comprehensive interview report via AI.
nodes.name: route.ts
POST
nodes.summary: Generate interview report from candidate name, job title, and Q&A array with scores.
Interview start endpoint: resolves candidate from mock data or request body, builds rich CV context string, generates interview questions via AI. Supports both mock and dynamic candidates.
nodes.name: route.ts
POST
nodes.summary: Start interview: resolve candidate, build CV context, generate greeting + questions via AI.

nodes.name: getJdForCandidate
nodes.summary: Map candidate ID prefix to appropriate JD title and description (ai- prefix vs default).
JD parse trigger endpoint: kicks off Gemini AI parsing of job description text. Requires manage:jobs permission.
nodes.name: route.ts
POST
nodes.summary: Trigger JD parsing via Gemini AI; validates JD exists and has content.
JD requirements CRUD: GET lists requirements for a JD, POST adds manual requirement with Zod validation. Uses Prisma directly for create.
nodes.name: route.ts
GET
nodes.summary: List requirements for a specific JD with view:jobs permission.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Add manual requirement to JD with Zod validation and manage:jobs permission.
REST API for single JD: GET (detail with requirements), PUT (update with Zod), DELETE (soft delete/archive). Auth + RBAC enforced.
nodes.name: route.ts
GET
nodes.summary: Fetch single JD by ID with requirements, view:jobs permission.

nodes.name: PUT
nodes.summary: Update JD fields with Zod validation and manage:jobs permission.

nodes.name: DELETE
nodes.summary: Soft-delete (archive) JD with manage:jobs permission.
Generate mock candidates for a JD using Gemini AI. Accepts title, department, description, count. No auth required.
nodes.name: route.ts
POST
nodes.summary: Generate AI mock candidates for a JD given title and optional params.
REST API for JD collection: GET (paginated list with status/search filters) and POST (create new JD). Auth + RBAC enforced.
nodes.name: route.ts
GET
nodes.summary: Paginated JD list with page/limit/status/search filters and view:jobs permission.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Create new JD with Zod validation and manage:jobs permission.
Current user profile endpoint: GET returns user profile, PATCH updates firstName/lastName/company with Zod validation. Auth via session only (no RBAC).
nodes.name: route.ts
GET
nodes.summary: Fetch current authenticated user profile from Prisma.

nodes.name: PATCH
nodes.summary: Update current user's firstName, lastName, company with Zod validation.

auth

Google Generative AI client wrapper. GeminiClient class with methods: getModel, generateContent, generateJSON (with JSON extraction fallback), generateEmbedding (text-embedding-004). Singleton via globalThis pattern. Uses GEMINI_API_KEY env var.
nodes.name: gemini.ts
GeminiClient
nodes.summary: Wrapper around GoogleGenerativeAI SDK. Provides getModel(), pro/flash getters, generateContent(), generateJSON<T>() with JSON parse fallback, generateEmbedding() using text-embedding-004.

nodes.label: mock-ai-engineer-sourcing.ts
nodes.summary: Mock data for CV Sourcing demo with 8 AI Engineer candidates for NovaGroup. Contains JD title/department/description constants and candidate array with scoring breakdowns (keyword, experience, education, language, AI).
nodes.name: mock-ai-engineer-sourcing.ts

nodes.label: mock-candidates-sourcing.ts
nodes.summary: Defines MockSourcingCandidate interface (id, fullName, scores, skills, scoreBreakdown) and exports 12 mock purchasing role candidates. Also exports SOURCE_LABELS, STATUS_OPTIONS, STATUS_LABELS, STATUS_COLORS for UI display.
nodes.name: mock-candidates-sourcing.ts

nodes.label: MockSourcingCandidate
nodes.summary: Interface for sourcing candidates: id, fullName, currentTitle, currentCompany, experienceYears, overallScore, matchedSkills, missingSkills, totalSkills, sourcePlatform (linkedin|topcv|vnworks|upload), sourceUrl, status (scored|shortlisted|rejected|contacted), cvSummary, scoreBreakdown (keyword/experience/education/language/ai).
nodes.name: MockSourcingCandidate

nodes.label: prisma.ts
nodes.summary: Prisma client singleton using PrismaPg adapter. Creates PrismaClient with DATABASE_URL, caches on globalThis in non-production to survive HMR. Exports prisma instance as named and default export.
nodes.name: prisma.ts

nodes.label: createPrismaClient
nodes.summary: Creates PrismaClient with PrismaPg adapter from DATABASE_URL env var. Enables query/error/warn logging in dev, error only in prod.
nodes.name: createPrismaClient

nodes.label: rbac.ts
nodes.summary: Role-based access control module. Defines Permission type union (10 permissions), ROLE_PERMISSIONS map for ADMIN/RECRUITER/CANDIDATE roles. Exports hasPermission, requireRole, requirePermission functions for authorization checks.
nodes.name: rbac.ts

nodes.label: hasPermission
nodes.summary: Checks if a UserRole has a specific Permission by looking up ROLE_PERMISSIONS map.
nodes.name: hasPermission

nodes.label: requireRole
nodes.summary: Throws if session user doesn't have one of the required UserRole values. Guards route handlers.
nodes.name: requireRole

nodes.label: requirePermission
nodes.summary: Throws if session user's role lacks the specified Permission. Guards route handlers.
nodes.name: requirePermission

nodes.label: redis.ts
nodes.summary: Redis client singleton using ioredis. Connects via REDIS_URL env var (defaults localhost:6379). Caches on globalThis in non-production. Configures maxRetries, readyCheck, lazyConnect. Logs connect/error events.
nodes.name: redis.ts

nodes.label: createRedisClient
nodes.summary: Creates ioredis Redis client with REDIS_URL, maxRetries=3, lazyConnect, error/connect event handlers.
nodes.name: createRedisClient

nodes.label: candidate-generator.ts
nodes.summary: AI-powered mock candidate generator using Gemini. Takes JD title/department/description/count, prompts Gemini to generate realistic Vietnamese candidates with scoring breakdowns. Parses JSON response and maps to MockSourcingCandidate format.
nodes.name: candidate-generator.ts

nodes.label: generateCandidatesForJd
nodes.summary: Generates mock candidates via Gemini AI for a given JD. Builds Vietnamese prompt, calls gemini.generateContent with flash-lite model, parses JSON response, maps to MockSourcingCandidate[] with generated IDs and validated fields.
nodes.name: generateCandidatesForJd

nodes.label: cv-parser.ts
nodes.summary: CV parsing service using Gemini AI. Takes raw CV text, sends to gemini.generateJSON with structured extraction prompt, validates output against parsedCvDataSchema (Zod). Falls back to partial data with defaults on validation error.
nodes.name: cv-parser.ts

nodes.label: parseCvText
nodes.summary: Parses raw CV text via Gemini AI (flash-lite, temp=0.3). Validates result with parsedCvDataSchema Zod schema. Returns ParsedCvData with personalInfo, skills, experience, education, certifications, languages.
nodes.name: parseCvText

nodes.label: cv-service.ts
nodes.summary: Full CV and candidate management service. CRUD ops for candidates via Prisma (create, list with pagination/search/filter, getById, update, softDelete). CV upload with file storage, PDF text extraction (pdf-parse), auto-parse via cv-parser, skill creation in DB transaction.
nodes.name: cv-service.ts

nodes.label: createCandidate
nodes.summary: Creates a candidate record in DB via Prisma with firstName, lastName, email, phone, source, notes.
nodes.name: createCandidate

nodes.label: getCandidates
nodes.summary: Lists candidates with pagination, status filter, search (name/email), and JD filter. Returns items with cv/interview counts and pagination metadata.
nodes.name: getCandidates

nodes.label: getCandidateById
nodes.summary: Fetches single candidate with CVs and interviews (including job title) by ID.
nodes.name: getCandidateById

nodes.label: uploadAndParseCv
nodes.summary: Handles CV file upload: saves to disk with UUID name, creates CV record in DB, extracts text from PDF (pdf-parse) or plain text, fires background parse via parseCvText. Returns CV record and parse status.
nodes.name: uploadAndParseCv

nodes.label: parseExistingCv
nodes.summary: Re-parses an existing CV by reading file from disk, extracting text, and calling parseCvRecord.
nodes.name: parseExistingCv

nodes.label: parseCvRecord
nodes.summary: Internal function: sets CV status to PROCESSING, calls parseCvText AI, deletes old skills, creates new CV data and skills in a Prisma transaction. Handles failure by setting FAILED status.
nodes.name: parseCvRecord

nodes.label: evaluation-rubric-generator.ts
nodes.summary: Generates interview evaluation rubrics from parsed JD data. Creates weighted dimensions (Technical Skills, Problem Solving, Behavioral, Culture Fit) with criteria descriptions (excellent/good/poor). Estimates question count and interview duration.
nodes.name: evaluation-rubric-generator.ts

nodes.label: EvaluationRubric
nodes.summary: Interface: dimensions array (name, weight 0-1, criteria with excellent/good/poor descriptions), totalQuestions count, estimatedDuration in minutes.
nodes.name: EvaluationRubric

nodes.label: generateEvaluationRubric
nodes.summary: Converts parsed JD interviewFocusAreas percentages into weighted evaluation dimensions with criteria. Calculates total questions from requirements count and estimates ~3.5 min per question duration.
nodes.name: generateEvaluationRubric

nodes.label: interview-ai-service.ts
nodes.summary: Core interview AI service using Gemini. Generates personalized interview questions from CV+JD, evaluates candidate answers with scoring, produces natural AI responses between questions, and generates comprehensive interview reports with scores and recommendations.
nodes.name: interview-ai-service.ts

nodes.label: GeneratedQuestion
nodes.summary: Interface: questionText, category (technical|experience|behavioral|situational), difficulty (EASY|MEDIUM|HARD).
nodes.name: GeneratedQuestion

nodes.label: AnswerEvaluation
nodes.summary: Interface: score (0-10), feedback (Vietnamese), strengths array, improvements array.
nodes.name: AnswerEvaluation

nodes.label: InterviewReportData
nodes.summary: Interface: overallScore, scoreTechnical, scoreExperience, scoreCommunication, scoreProblemSolving, attitude, recommendation (STRONGLY_RECOMMEND|RECOMMEND|CONSIDER|REJECT), recommendationReason, strengths, weaknesses.
nodes.name: InterviewReportData

nodes.label: generateInterviewQuestions
nodes.summary: Generates personalized greeting + 5 interview questions from CV summary and JD via Gemini. Questions span technical, experience, behavioral, situational categories with increasing difficulty.
nodes.name: generateInterviewQuestions

nodes.label: evaluateAnswer
nodes.summary: Evaluates a candidate's answer to an interview question via Gemini. Returns score 0-10, Vietnamese feedback, strengths and improvements.
nodes.name: evaluateAnswer

nodes.label: generateAIResponse
nodes.summary: Generates natural conversational AI response between interview questions. Provides brief positive feedback on answer then transitions to next question, or closes interview if last question.
nodes.name: generateAIResponse

nodes.label: generateInterviewReport
nodes.summary: Generates comprehensive interview report from all Q&A pairs via Gemini. Produces overall/category scores, attitude assessment, hire recommendation with reason, strengths and weaknesses.
nodes.name: generateInterviewReport

nodes.summary: Parses raw job description text using Gemini AI into structured ParsedJdData with position info, requirements, responsibilities, keywords, and interview focus areas. Normalizes focus area percentages to sum to 100.
nodes.name: jd-parser.ts
parseJobDescription
nodes.summary: Sends raw JD text to Gemini, returns ParsedJdData. Normalizes interviewFocusAreas percentages to sum 100.

nodes.name: ParsedJdData
nodes.summary: Interface for structured JD data: position metadata, technical/soft/education requirements, responsibilities, keywords, interview focus area weights.

CRUD service for job positions using Prisma. Supports paginated listing, create/update/soft-delete, and AI-powered parse-and-update flow that generates requirements and evaluation rubrics from raw JD text.
nodes.name: jd-service.ts
getJobs
nodes.summary: Paginated listing of job positions with optional status/search filters. Returns items + pagination metadata.

nodes.name: getJobById
nodes.summary: Fetches single job position by ID with requirements included.

nodes.name: createJob
nodes.summary: Creates a new job position. Auto-triggers parseAndUpdateJob if rawJdText is provided.

nodes.name: updateJob
nodes.summary: Partial update of job position fields.

nodes.name: deleteJob
nodes.summary: Soft-deletes a job by setting status to ARCHIVED.

nodes.name: parseAndUpdateJob
nodes.summary: Orchestrates AI parsing: calls parseJobDescription + generateEvaluationRubric, deletes old requirements, creates new ones in a Prisma transaction.

nodes.name: buildRequirementsFromParsed
nodes.summary: Transforms ParsedJdData into array of job requirement records (TECHNICAL, SOFT, EXPERIENCE, EDUCATION, CERTIFICATION) with weights.

Scores candidate-JD match using keyword matching for technical skills, experience, and education, with weighted overall score (70% must-have, 30% nice-to-have). Uses Gemini for semantic strengths/gaps analysis with keyword fallback.
nodes.name: matching-engine.ts
calculateMatch
nodes.summary: Computes MatchResult from parsed JD and CV: keyword-based scoring for tech/exp/edu, Gemini semantic analysis for strengths/gaps, weighted overall score.

nodes.name: keywordMatch
nodes.summary: Case-insensitive bidirectional substring matching between required and available skill lists. Returns matched/missing arrays.

nodes.name: MatchResult
nodes.summary: Interface for match scoring result: overallScore, breakdown (tech/exp/edu/niceToHave), strengths, gaps, recommendedFocusAreas.
Utility module exporting cn() function that merges class names using clsx and tailwind-merge.
nodes.name: utils.ts
cn
nodes.summary: Merges CSS class names with clsx and tailwind-merge for Tailwind conflict resolution.

Zod validation schemas for CV operations: uploadCvSchema (file metadata), parseCvSchema, cvSkillSchema, parsedCvDataSchema (personal info, skills, experience, education, certifications, languages). Exports inferred types.
nodes.name: cv.ts
uploadCvSchema
nodes.summary: Zod schema validating CV upload: candidateId, fileName, fileSize (max 10MB), mimeType (pdf/text), fileUrl.

nodes.name: parsedCvDataSchema
nodes.summary: Zod schema for parsed CV data structure: personalInfo, summary, skills, experience, education, certifications, languages, totalYearsExperience.
Zod validation schemas for job position CRUD: createJobPositionSchema, updateJobPositionSchema (partial), createJobRequirementSchema, parseJdSchema. Exports inferred types.
nodes.name: jd.ts
createJobPositionSchema
nodes.summary: Zod schema for creating job positions: title, department, location, employmentType enum, description, rawJdText, interviewSettings.

nodes.name: createJobRequirementSchema
nodes.summary: Zod schema for job requirements: jobId, type enum, name, description, level, yearsRequired, isRequired, weight.
Next.js middleware for route protection. Defines public/protected paths but currently runs in demo mode (allows all requests). Exports matcher config excluding static assets.
nodes.name: proxy.ts
proxy
nodes.summary: Middleware function; currently demo mode — passes all requests through without authentication.

Zustand store managing interview flow state machine (idle→loading→greeting→questioning→evaluating→closing→report). Handles chat messages, question progression, answer evaluation via API, and report generation.
nodes.name: interview-store.ts
useInterviewStore
nodes.summary: Zustand store with actions: startInterview (fetches greeting+questions), advanceFromGreeting, sendAnswer (evaluates via API, advances), endInterview (generates report), reset.

nodes.name: InterviewStep
nodes.summary: Union type for interview flow steps: idle | loading | greeting | questioning | evaluating | closing | generating-report | report.

nodes.name: ChatMessage
nodes.summary: Interface for chat messages: id, role (ai|candidate), content, timestamp.
Zustand store for dynamic JD-to-candidates sourcing. Manages JD entries with statuses (idle→sourcing→scoring→done), calls API to generate candidates via AI, animates scoring progress reveal.
nodes.name: sourcing-store.ts
useSourcingStore
nodes.summary: Zustand store with createAndSource (API call + scoring animation), getJd, getAllCandidates. Manages JdEntry[] with progress tracking.

nodes.name: JdEntry
nodes.summary: Interface for sourcing JD entry: id, title, department, description, candidates, status, scoringProgress, scoredCount, createdAt.
NextAuth type augmentations: adds 'role: UserRole' to User, Session.user, and JWT interfaces. Re-exports UserRole from generated Prisma client.
nodes.name: auth.ts

layers.name: UI Pages & Feature Components
layers.description: Next.js App Router pages (dashboard, candidates, interviews, jobs, reports) and feature-specific React components (candidate, interview, jd, layout, sourcing). Consumes UI primitives, stores, and services.

layers.name: UI Primitives
layers.description: Shared shadcn/ui component library built on @base-ui/react primitives (Button, Card, Badge, Table, Dialog, Select, Sidebar, etc.). Reusable across all pages and feature components.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Next.js API route handlers for REST endpoints (auth, candidates, CVs, interviews, JDs, health, users). Enforces auth/RBAC and delegates to service layer.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Core business logic services: CV parsing, JD parsing, candidate generation, matching engine, interview AI, evaluation rubrics. Orchestrates AI (Gemini) and database operations.

layers.name: Client State Layer
layers.description: Zustand stores (interview flow state machine, sourcing progress) and React hooks (mobile detection). Manages client-side application state.

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: Foundation modules: auth (NextAuth), database (Prisma client), AI client (Gemini), Redis, RBAC, middleware, Zod validation schemas, TypeScript type declarations, and mock data.

layers.name: Configuration & Testing
layers.description: Root configuration files (ESLint, Next.js, PostCSS, Prisma config) and Puppeteer-based UI test scripts for TTS sync, sourcing, and design scraping.

tour.title: App Entry Point — Root Layout
tour.description: The Next.js root layout bootstraps the entire application. It sets up Geist fonts, configures Vietnamese locale metadata for NovaGroup HR AI Interview, and wraps all pages in a Providers component. This is the outermost shell that every route renders inside.
tour.languageLesson: In Next.js App Router, layout.tsx files define persistent UI wrappers. The root layout in src/app/ applies to every page in the application.

tour.title: Dashboard Shell — Sidebar and Header
tour.description: The dashboard layout wraps all authenticated pages with a sidebar navigation and top header bar. The AppSidebar provides navigation links to Jobs, Candidates, Interviews, and Reports sections. The AppHeader displays breadcrumbs and user profile controls. Together they form the main chrome the user interacts with after login.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js route groups like (dashboard) let you apply a shared layout to a set of routes without affecting the URL path. The parentheses are stripped from the URL.

tour.title: Dashboard Overview — Landing Page
tour.description: The dashboard page is the first screen users see after authentication. It imports Card components and presents summary statistics for jobs, candidates, and interviews. This page acts as the hub from which HR managers navigate to specific workflows.

tour.title: Job Description Management
tour.description: The jobs section handles the full lifecycle of job descriptions: listing, creating, viewing details, and editing. The JD list page (fanOut=6) imports tables, badges, and buttons to present all positions. Creating a new JD feeds into the AI parsing pipeline explored in the next step. JD-specific components render parsed summaries and status badges.

tour.title: JD Parsing and API Routes
tour.description: When a job description is created or updated, API routes handle the server-side processing. The JD parse route invokes the JD parser service which uses Google Gemini AI to extract structured requirements from free-text job descriptions. The JD service orchestrates CRUD operations against Prisma and delegates AI analysis to the parser.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js API routes in the App Router use route.ts files exporting HTTP method handlers (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE). They run server-side only and can safely access databases and external APIs.

tour.title: Candidate Management and CV Upload
tour.description: The candidates section is the most connected page area in the app (fanOut=11 for the list page). It displays candidates in tables with status badges, allows creating new candidates with CV uploads, and shows individual candidate profiles. The CV upload zone component handles file selection and upload UX, while the CV parsed result component displays AI-extracted resume data.

tour.title: CV Parsing and Candidate Services
tour.description: Behind the candidate UI, the CV parser service uses Gemini AI to extract structured data from uploaded resumes. The CV service manages storage and retrieval of CVs linked to candidates. The candidate generator can produce synthetic candidate profiles for testing. Validation schemas ensure CV data conforms to expected structures before persistence.

tour.title: AI Matching Engine
tour.description: The matching engine is the core intelligence that scores candidates against job requirements. The match API route (fanOut=6, the highest of any API route) orchestrates the scoring by importing the matching engine service, Gemini client, Prisma, auth, and validation schemas. The match score card component renders results visually with progress indicators. This step connects the JD and candidate data pipelines from previous steps.

tour.title: Interview Flow — Conducting AI Interviews
tour.description: The interview section manages the full interview lifecycle. The interview list page shows scheduled and completed interviews. The interview detail page (fanOut=5) is where the actual AI interview happens, integrating chat, video avatar, and state management. The interview store (fanIn=5, a Zustand store) manages the interview state machine tracking phases like intro, questioning, and evaluation.

tour.title: Chat Interface and Video Avatar
tour.description: The chat interface component (fanOut=5) orchestrates the real-time conversation between the AI interviewer and candidate. It renders message bubbles with a typing indicator for streaming responses. The video avatar panel displays an animated AI interviewer face with TTS-synchronized mouth movements, creating a more engaging interview experience. The interview transcript component logs the full conversation history.

tour.title: Interview AI Service and Evaluation
tour.description: The interview AI service powers the conversational logic, generating contextual follow-up questions based on the candidate's responses and the job requirements. The evaluation rubric generator creates scoring criteria tailored to each position. The report page and report card component present the final interview assessment. These services connect the AI backbone from the matching engine to produce end-to-end hiring intelligence.

tour.title: Infrastructure — Auth, Database, and AI Client
tour.description: The infrastructure layer forms the foundation everything else depends on. The utils module (fanIn=23) is the most imported file, providing shared helpers like the cn() classname merger. The Prisma client (fanIn=5) manages all database access. The Gemini client (fanIn=5) configures the Google AI connection used by all AI services. Auth (fanIn=4) and RBAC modules enforce access control across API routes.
tour.languageLesson: The fanIn metric (how many files import a module) reveals the most critical infrastructure. Breaking changes in high-fanIn files like utils.ts (23 importers) or button.tsx (19 importers) have the widest blast radius.
